message:
Lazy JSON parsing: zero-copy strings via COW (7.9s, 1.29x Rust)

json.loads() now uses lazy mode:
- Strings without escapes borrow from source JSON (zero-copy)
- Source kept alive via PyString COW refcount
- Only copies when string has escape sequences

Performance improvement:
- Before: 10.5s (1.71x slower than Rust)
- After: 7.9s (1.29x slower than Rust)
- 25% faster overall

Changes:
- parse_direct: Added parseWithSource() with lazy_source threadlocal
- string.zig: Use createBorrowed() when lazy_source set
- json.zig: loads() now passes source for lazy mode

-*PyAOT is 1.33x slower than Rust due to PyObject allocation overhead required for C API compatibility (NumPy, etc.)*
+*PyAOT is 1.29x slower than Rust due to PyObject allocation overhead required for C API compatibility (NumPy, etc.)*
